items.name: GoCoMo Transit — Black Route (#1)
items.description: One of two high-frequency 'connector' routes running a large north/south loop through the heart of Columbia. Runs in both directions and connects campus to downtown and north Columbia.

items.name: GoCoMo Transit — Gold Route (#3)
items.description: One of two high-frequency 'connector' routes running a large east/west loop through the city. High-frequency service in both directions. Connects campus to east and west Columbia.

items.name: GoCoMo Transit — Neighborhood Routes (#2, #4, #5, #6)
items.description: Four smaller neighborhood loop routes (Red, Orange, Blue, Green) serving areas not covered by the connector routes, including Lake of the Woods and Scott Blvd. Lower frequency than connector routes.
stops: Varies by route — see gocomotransit.com for maps

items.name: GoCoMo Game Day Routes
items.description: Special bus routes that run on Mizzou home football game days, providing extra service between campus, parking areas, and Memorial Stadium.
items.hours: Game days only — schedule posted at gocomotransit.com

items.name: Campus Parking
items.description: Student parking permits are available for on-campus lots. Permit zones are enforced. Summer 2026 permits: Tier 1 lots $126, Tier 2 lots $60. Fall semester rates vary — check parking.missouri.edu for current pricing and lot maps.
items.hours: Enforcement hours vary by zone — check permit area signage

items.name: Bike Share — VeoRide
items.description: Dockless e-bikes and e-scooters available throughout campus and downtown Columbia. Unlock via the VeoRide app. Low per-minute cost; a good option for short trips between class buildings.
